proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_009` == 1 have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `feature_009` == 0.
Cell lines with `feature_022` == 1 have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `feature_022` == 0.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample test of dependency_BRAF for feature_009 == 1 versus all other cell lines; effect is subgroup mean minus complement mean.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_BRAF` averaged -0.805 in `feature_009` == 1 lines (n=5574) versus -0.250 in other lines; the subgroup was much more BRAF-dependent.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample test of dependency_EGFR for feature_022 == 1 versus all other cell lines; effect is subgroup mean minus complement mean.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_EGFR` averaged -0.601 in `feature_022` == 1 lines (n=3880) versus -0.252 in other lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_024` == 1 have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `feature_024` == 0.
Cell lines with `feature_014` == 1 have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `feature_014` == 0.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample test of dependency_ERBB2 for feature_024 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_ERBB2` averaged -0.686 in `feature_024` == 1 lines (n=4890) versus -0.253 in other lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample test of dependency_KRAS for feature_014 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KRAS` averaged -0.559 in `feature_014` == 1 lines (n=11842) versus -0.252 in other lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_027` == 1 have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `feature_027` == 0.
Increasing `feature_016` is associated with more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample test of dependency_RIT1 for feature_027 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RIT1` averaged -0.353 in `feature_027` == 1 lines (n=4560) versus -0.257 in other lines.

analyses.code: Univariate linear slope of dependency_RIT1 on continuous feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: Each 1-unit increase in `feature_016` was associated with a -0.0881 shift in `dependency_RIT1`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Increasing `feature_016` is associated with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores.
Increasing `feature_016` is associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores.
Increasing `feature_016` is associated with more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores.

analyses.code: Univariate linear slope of dependency_POLR2A on continuous feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest continuous `feature_016` signal was for `dependency_POLR2A`, with a -0.3048 score shift per 1-unit increase.

analyses.code: Univariate linear slope of dependency_TMED10 on continuous feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_TMED10` became modestly more negative as `feature_016` increased, slope -0.1012 per unit.

analyses.code: Univariate linear slope of dependency_DHX9 on continuous feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_DHX9` became modestly more negative as `feature_016` increased, slope -0.1086 per unit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Increasing `feature_003` is associated with more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores.
Cell lines with `feature_018` == 1 have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with `feature_018` == 0.

analyses.code: Univariate linear slope of dependency_RPL11 on continuous feature_003.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RPL11` became more negative as `feature_003` increased; slope was -0.1015 per 1-unit increase.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample test of dependency_RPL11 for feature_018 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RPL11` averaged -0.361 in `feature_018` == 1 lines (n=8055) versus -0.252 in other lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_011` == 'colorectal' have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_035` == 1 have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `feature_035` == 0.
Cell lines in the high quartile of `feature_028` (`feature_028` >= 0.819) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample test of dependency_KIF18A for feature_011 == colorectal versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` averaged -0.720 in colorectal lines (n=5816) versus -0.252 in other lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample test of dependency_KIF18A for feature_035 == 1 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` averaged -0.545 in `feature_035` == 1 lines (n=9285) versus -0.253 in other lines.

analyses.code: Welch two-sample test of dependency_KIF18A for feature_028 >= 0.819 versus all other cell lines.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` averaged -0.469 in the high-`feature_028` quartile (n=12508) versus -0.253 in other lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_011` == 'colorectal' and `feature_035` == 1 have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than other cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_011` == 'colorectal' and high `feature_028` (`feature_028` >= 0.819) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than other cell lines.

The strongest `dependency_KIF18A` dependency is concentrated in cell lines with `feature_011` == 'colorectal', `feature_035` == 1, and high `feature_028` (`feature_028` >= 0.819); this subgroup has more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup search; Welch test for feature_011 == colorectal and feature_035 == 1.
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/`feature_035` == 1 subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.943 (n=3925) versus -0.253 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup search; Welch test for feature_011 == colorectal and feature_028 >= 0.819.
analyses.result_summary: The colorectal/high-`feature_028` subgroup had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -0.929 (n=4006) versus -0.253 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Systematic pair and top-triple subgroup search across candidate predicates for dependency_KIF18A; Welch test for the best triple.
analyses.result_summary: The complete triple subgroup (`feature_011` == colorectal, `feature_035` == 1, `feature_028` >= 0.819) had mean `dependency_KIF18A` -1.112 (n=3133) versus -0.253 in all other lines, a larger effect than any component or two-predicate subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the systematic heterogeneity screen, the subgroup with `feature_009` == 1 and `feature_021` == 0 has more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, the subgroup with `feature_022` == 1 and `feature_021` == 0 has more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, the subgroup with `feature_024` == 1 and `feature_021` == 0 has more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, the subgroup with `feature_014` == 1 and `feature_021` == 0 has more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, the subgroup with `feature_027` == 1 and `feature_022` == 0 has more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Exhaustive two-predicate dependency-heterogeneity search; Welch test for feature_009 == 1 and feature_021 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_009` == 1 and `feature_021` == 0 subgroup had mean `dependency_BRAF` -0.803 (n=5279) versus -0.253 in all other lines; this did not materially exceed the `feature_009` main effect.

analyses.code: Exhaustive two-predicate dependency-heterogeneity search; Welch test for feature_022 == 1 and feature_021 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_022` == 1 and `feature_021` == 0 subgroup had mean `dependency_EGFR` -0.602 (n=3672) versus -0.254 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Exhaustive two-predicate dependency-heterogeneity search; Welch test for feature_024 == 1 and feature_021 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_024` == 1 and `feature_021` == 0 subgroup had mean `dependency_ERBB2` -0.684 (n=4647) versus -0.256 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Exhaustive two-predicate dependency-heterogeneity search; Welch test for feature_014 == 1 and feature_021 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_014` == 1 and `feature_021` == 0 subgroup had mean `dependency_KRAS` -0.559 (n=11218) versus -0.257 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Exhaustive two-predicate dependency-heterogeneity search; Welch test for feature_027 == 1 and feature_022 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_027` == 1 and `feature_022` == 0 subgroup had mean `dependency_RIT1` -0.356 (n=4205) versus -0.258 in all other lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the systematic heterogeneity screen, the subgroup with high `feature_016` (`feature_016` >= 0.964) and `feature_024` == 0 has more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, the subgroup with high `feature_016` (`feature_016` >= 0.964) and `feature_009` == 0 has more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than all other cell lines.
In the systematic heterogeneity screen, the subgroup with high `feature_016` (`feature_016` >= 0.964) and `feature_027` == 0 has more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than all other cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_018` == 1 and high `feature_003` (`feature_003` >= 0.428) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for dependency_TMED10; Welch test for feature_016 >= 0.964 and feature_024 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: The high-`feature_016`/`feature_024` == 0 subgroup had mean `dependency_TMED10` -0.283 (n=11330) versus -0.245 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for dependency_DHX9; Welch test for feature_016 >= 0.964 and feature_009 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: The high-`feature_016`/`feature_009` == 0 subgroup had mean `dependency_DHX9` -0.363 (n=11175) versus -0.325 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Systematic dependency-heterogeneity search for dependency_POLR2A; Welch test for feature_016 >= 0.964 and feature_027 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: The high-`feature_016`/`feature_027` == 0 subgroup had mean `dependency_POLR2A` -0.500 (n=11421) versus -0.395 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Manual follow-up to the systematic heterogeneity search; Welch test for feature_018 == 1 and feature_003 >= 0.428.
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_018` == 1/high-`feature_003` subgroup had mean `dependency_RPL11` -0.365 (n=7797) versus -0.252 in all other lines.
